hero.title: Your Path to a
hero.subtitle: Build academic foundations from Grade 1 or earn your Ontario Secondary School Diploma (OSSD) 100% online from anywhere in the world. Study at your own pace with Ontario Certified Teachers.

programPathways.subtitle: Choose the learning path that fits your goals

elementary.title: Elementary Foundation (Grades 1-5)
elementary.desc: Build essential academic skills aligned with the Ontario Curriculum. Perfect for international students preparing for Canadian studies or families seeking curriculum-aligned learning support.

middle.desc: Strengthen foundations for high school success with Ontario curriculum-aligned courses.

highSchoolAcademicPrep.title: High School Preparation (Grades 9 to 12)
highSchoolAcademicPrep.desc: Non-credit, skills-based preparation that readies students to upgrade into the Official Ontario Program or into an EAP pathway.

programPathways.footnote: Registration and entrance test fees vary by grade band. Grades 1 to 5 have no entrance test fee; Grades 6 to 12 have a $50 CAD entrance test fee. Grades 1 to 8 have a $50 CAD registration fee; Grades 9 to 12 have a $100 CAD registration fee. All fees are non-refundable.

why.title: Why Choose EMCS?
why.subtitle: We make Canadian education accessible, flexible, and supported—wherever you are in the world.

card1.title: Study From Anywhere
card1.desc: Learn from Vietnam, Canada, or anywhere globally. All you need is an internet connection. No study permit required for online courses.

card2.title: Your Schedule, Your Pace
card2.desc: Start anytime—we offer continuous enrollment year-round. Complete courses in as little as 4 weeks or take up to 12 months.

card3.title: Ontario Certified Teachers
card3.desc: All credit courses are delivered by teachers in good standing with the Ontario College of Teachers (OCT), with real classroom experience.

card4.title: Globally Recognized Credentials
card4.desc: Our OSSD credits are the same as those earned in traditional Ontario schools—recognized by universities in Canada, the US, UK, Australia, and worldwide.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.subtitle: Getting started is simple—register today and begin learning within 24-48 hours.

step1.title: 1. Register Anytime
step1.desc: Sign up 365 days a year. No deadlines, no waitlists. Continuous enrollment means you start when you're ready.

step2.title: 2. Learn at Your Pace
step2.desc: Access course materials 24/7. Our asynchronous platform fits your schedule—study when it works for you.

step3.title: 3. Get Teacher Support
step3.desc: Ontario Certified Teachers respond to questions within 48 hours. Office hours available for real-time help.

step4.title: 4. Earn Your Credential
step4.desc: Credits appear on official Ontario transcripts. Marks sent directly to OUAC, OCAS, and your day school.

primaryFoundation.title: Ontario Primary Foundation Program
primaryFoundation.desc: Grades 1 to 5 aligned with Ontario Curriculum. Two pathways available:
primaryFoundation.bullet1: Academic Preparation (Self-Learning, Non-Ontario student record): From $75 CAD per subject with the 50% promotional discount, or $325 CAD for the full-year 6-subject bundle.
primaryFoundation.bullet2: Upgrade to Ontario Official Program (Ontario student record): $250 CAD per course, or $600 CAD per year for the full 6-subject bundle.
primaryFoundation.bullet3: Live Teacher-Led Primary Foundation (with Ontario report card): $3,500 CAD per year (premium teacher-led pathway).

middleSchool.title: Middle School Foundation (Grades 6 to 8)
middleSchool.desc: Grades 6 to 8 Ontario Curriculum-aligned preparation with an optional upgrade to an Ontario student record.
middleSchool.bullet1: Academic Preparation (Non-Credit): $75 per course (50% off), $325 CAD for the full-year 6-subject bundle.
middleSchool.bullet2: Upgrade to Ontario Official Program (Ontario student record): $250 per course, $600 CAD per year.
middleSchool.bullet3: Entrance Test Fee: $50 CAD (non-refundable). Registration Fee: $50 CAD.

highSchool.title: Ontario High School Pathways (Grades 9 to 12)
highSchool.tagline: Flexible Learning Pathways. Learn First. Decide Later. Succeed Safely.
highSchool.desc: Two pathways for Grades 9 to 12, both aligned with the Ontario Curriculum. Start with the Academic Preparation Program for skills and readiness, then upgrade to the Official Ontario Program for credit-bearing courses and the OSSD.
highSchool.bullet1: Academic Preparation Program (Non-Credit): Skills-focused preparation with no Ontario student record.
highSchool.bullet2: Official Ontario Program: Credit-bearing, Ontario student record, leading to the Ontario Secondary School Diploma.
highSchool.bullet3: Registration Fee: $100 CAD. Entrance Test Fee: $50 CAD.
highSchool.bullet4: Grade 9 and Grade 10 = 8-credit one-year programs. Grade 11 and Grade 12 = 7-credit one-year programs.

home.heroSubtitleV2: We offer Ontario Curriculum-aligned learning for students in Grades 1 to 12 through two flexible pathways: Academic Preparation Program (Non-Ontario student record) and Official Ontario Program (Ontario student record). Students may start with preparation learning and upgrade to credit-bearing courses when academically ready.
home.taglineLearn: Learn First. Decide Later. Succeed Safely.

accreditation.title: Ministry Inspected Private School
accreditation.statement: EMCS is inspected by the Ontario Ministry of Education and authorized to grant credits toward the Ontario Secondary School Diploma (OSSD).
accreditation.details: Our credits hold the same value as those earned in traditional Ontario day schools and are recognized by all Canadian colleges and universities. Transcripts are sent directly to OUAC, OCAS, and educational institutions worldwide.

programDropdown.leftTagline: Self-paced preparation. Learn First. Decide Later.
programDropdown.leftDescription: The Academic Preparation Program builds strong foundations in Academic English, mathematics, science, social studies, and essential learning skills. Aligned with Ontario standards, it develops analytical thinking and disciplined study habits, preparing students for a smooth transition into OSSD credit courses or the Ontario Secondary School Diploma (OSSD) program.
programDropdown.rightTagline: Earn official OSSD credits. Succeed Safely.
programDropdown.rightDescription: The Official Ontario Program is a fully accredited Canadian secondary education pathway designed for students pursuing academic excellence and global university opportunities. Aligned with Ontario curriculum standards, the program emphasizes strong academic foundations, critical thinking, and disciplined learning. With personalized academic guidance and a structured learning environment, students are prepared for university admission in Canada and internationally. Upon successful completion, students earn the Ontario Secondary School Diploma (OSSD).
programDropdown.footerTagline: Flexible Learning Pathways. Learn First. Decide Later. Succeed Safely.

footer.tagline: Ontario-accredited online education for students worldwide

about.title: About EMCS
about.subtitle: Toronto EMCS

whoWeAre.title: Who We Are
whoWeAre.content: EMCS is a Ministry-inspected private school in Toronto, authorized by the Ontario Ministry of Education to grant OSSD credits. We provide high-quality education for students from Grades 1-12 based on the official Ontario Curriculum, with a focus on flexible, online delivery and personalized student support.
whoWeAre.bsid: BSID: 665588 | Inspected by the Ontario Ministry of Education

about.storyText: EMCS was founded with a mission to bring world-class Canadian education to students around the globe. We recognized that geography should never be a barrier to quality education. Since our founding, we have helped thousands of students earn Ontario high school credits, complete their OSSD, and gain admission to top universities worldwide.
about.missionText: To provide flexible, high-quality Ontario curriculum education that empowers students to achieve their academic goals and gain admission to top universities worldwide. We strive to inspire and equip our students with the necessary mindset and skills to become independent lifelong learners, fully dedicated to making tangible and positive contributions to their communities and beyond.

philosophy.title: Our Teaching Philosophy
philosophy.content: Led by our motto, 'Teach for love. Learn for life', we are dedicated to fostering a dynamic community of individuals committed to lifelong learning. We embody the fundamental values and principles that prepare students for success in higher education and beyond.

about.accreditationText: We are a Ministry-inspected private school authorized by the Ontario Ministry of Education to grant Ontario Secondary School Diploma (OSSD) credits.

accreditationDetails.creditDetails: Same as traditional Ontario day schools

whatThisMeans.title: What This Means
whatThisMeans.point1: Our OSSD is the exact same diploma issued by traditional Ontario day schools
whatThisMeans.point2: Credits are recognized by all Canadian colleges and universities
whatThisMeans.point3: International students can earn Canadian credentials without a study permit
whatThisMeans.point4: Marks are sent directly to OUAC and OCAS for post-secondary applications

excellence.title: Excellence
excellence.desc: We maintain the highest standards in curriculum delivery, assessment, and student support.

accessibility.title: Accessibility
accessibility.desc: Quality education should be available to all students, regardless of location or circumstances.

innovation.title: Innovation
innovation.desc: We leverage technology to create engaging and effective learning experiences that prepare students for the future.

support.title: Support
support.desc: Every student receives personalized guidance throughout their educational journey.

missionStatement.title: Our Mission
missionStatement.content: EMCS strives to inspire and equip our students with the necessary mindset and skills to become independent lifelong learners, fully dedicated to making tangible and positive contributions to their communities and beyond.

principalMessage.title: Principal's Message
principalMessage.content: As the School year comes to an end, I want to extend a warm gratitude to our students, faculty and staff for another wonderful year of learning and growth. Every day I have the privilege of watching our students overcome new challenges and persevere, as they strive towards their academic goals and career aspirations.

overview.purpose: Prepare students for future studies within the Ontario education system through a clear, structured, and academically sound learning pathway. Students study all subjects in English, supporting language development and early exposure to an international learning environment.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Find answers to common questions about our programs
faq.a1: Yes, EMCS is a Ministry-inspected private school authorized by the Ontario Ministry of Education to grant OSSD credits. Our BSID is 665588. Our credits hold the same value as those earned in traditional Ontario day schools.
faq.a2: We have continuous enrollment year-round. You can register at any time and typically start your course within 24-48 hours.
faq.q3: How long do I have to complete a course?
faq.a3: You have up to 12 months from enrollment to complete each course, though most students finish in 3-4 months. Fast-track options allow completion in as little as 4 weeks.
faq.q4: Are EMCS credits the same as regular Ontario high school credits?
faq.a4: Yes. Our credits are identical to those earned at traditional Ontario day schools. They appear on official Ontario transcripts and are recognized by all Canadian colleges and universities.
faq.a5: Yes, all credit courses require a proctored final exam worth 30% of your grade. You can write your exam online with a remote proctor or at an approved testing center.
faq.q6: How do I get my marks sent to universities?
faq.a6: We send midterm and final marks directly to OUAC, OCAS, and your day school. Simply provide your application numbers through our transcript request system.
faq.q7: Can I transfer credits from another school?
faq.a7: Yes! We accept transfer credits from Ontario and other Canadian schools. International students can also have their transcripts evaluated for credit equivalency.
faq.q8: What is the difference between Self-Learning and Teacher-Led?
faq.a8: Self-Learning provides access to curriculum-aligned materials for practice—no assessments, no report card, no teacher interaction. Teacher-Led includes live online classes with Canadian teachers, required assessments, and an official Ontario academic report card.
faq.q9: Does the Self-Learning program provide credits?
faq.a9: No. The Primary Foundation Self-Learning program does not provide OSSD credits (which only apply to Grades 9-12) or an Ontario academic record. For an official record, either choose the Teacher-Led option or add the Ontario Academic Record add-on ($250 per course) to your Self-Learning enrollment.
faq.q10: Is the Primary Foundation program accredited?
faq.a10: EMCS is an Ontario Ministry-inspected private school. The Teacher-Led Primary Foundation program includes official Ontario academic report cards. The Self-Learning program provides curriculum-aligned practice materials without formal credentialing.
faq.q11: Do practice courses give me credits?
faq.a11: No. Practice courses are designed for skill-building and extra practice only. They do NOT earn Ontario high school credits and will NOT appear on any transcript or academic record. If you need credits, please enroll in our Credit Courses.
faq.a12: Video lessons, interactive practice activities, and homework exercises. No tests, no grades, no assessments, and no teacher instruction.
faq.q13: Do I need a study permit?
faq.a13: No. You can take EMCS online courses from anywhere in the world without a Canadian study permit.
faq.a14: Yes. The Ontario Secondary School Diploma (OSSD) is recognized by universities worldwide, including institutions in Canada, the United States, United Kingdom, Australia, and many other countries.
faq.a15: You need a computer or tablet with internet access, a webcam for proctored exams, and basic software like a web browser and PDF reader. No special software purchases required.

cta.title: Still have questions?
cta.subtitle: Our team is here to help. Contact us and we'll get back to you within 24 hours.

academicPrep.heroDesc: The Academic Preparation Program (Non-Credit) is designed for students who aspire to pursue a rigorous academic pathway within an international educational framework. The program focuses on establishing strong academic foundations while cultivating analytical thinking, disciplined study habits, and the confidence required to succeed within the Canadian education system. Developed in alignment with Ontario curriculum standards, the program strengthens students' competencies in Academic English, mathematical reasoning, and advanced learning skills. Through personalized learning pathways and close academic mentorship, the program provides a solid transition into OSSD credit courses or EAP programs, preparing students to excel in high-quality, globally oriented academic environments.
academicPrep.gradeGroupElemDesc: Build strong foundations in core subjects
academicPrep.gradeGroupHsDesc: Practice and review for credit courses
academicPrep.included1Desc: High-quality instructional videos covering the Ontario curriculum
academicPrep.included4Desc: Learn anytime, anywhere at your own speed
academicPrep.included5Desc: Full access to all course materials for 12 months
academicPrep.included6Desc: Monitor your learning journey and achievements
academicPrep.browseByGradeSubtitle: Select a grade group to explore available courses
academicPrep.pricingElem: 💰 $75 per subject or $450 for full year (all 6 subjects)
academicPrep.pricingHs: 💰 $75 per course or $450 for full year
academicPrep.keyFeature1Desc: All content aligned with the official Ontario curriculum standards
academicPrep.keyFeature2Desc: No deadlines or schedules—complete courses when it works for you
academicPrep.keyFeature3Desc: Engaging video lessons with practice activities and homework
academicPrep.keyFeature4Desc: Quality education at a fraction of the cost of traditional programs
academicPrep.ctaDesc: Choose a grade level above to explore courses and start your journey

officialOntario.heroTitle: Official Ontario Program — Ontario Secondary School Diploma (OSSD)
officialOntario.heroDesc: The Ontario Secondary School Diploma (OSSD) Program is a prestigious, fully accredited Canadian secondary education pathway designed for students pursuing academic excellence and global university opportunities. Delivered in alignment with Ontario curriculum standards, the program emphasizes strong academic foundations, critical thinking, and disciplined learning. Through personalized academic guidance and a structured learning environment, students are fully prepared for graduation, university admission in Canada and internationally, and long-term success within a globally recognized education system.
officialOntario.ministryAuth: Authorized to grant credits toward the Ontario Secondary School Diploma (OSSD)
officialOntario.included1Desc: Interactive classes via Zoom with Ontario Certified Teachers
officialOntario.included2Desc: Recognized by universities worldwide, sent directly to OUAC/OCAS
officialOntario.included3Desc: Tests, assignments, and evaluations count toward your grade
officialOntario.included4Desc: Official Ontario report cards upon course completion
officialOntario.included5Desc: All textbooks, resources, and course materials provided
officialOntario.included6Desc: Direct access to your teacher for help and guidance
officialOntario.browseByGradeSubtitle: Select a grade group to explore available courses
officialOntario.pricingElem: 💰 $250 per subject or $600 for full year (all 6 subjects)
officialOntario.keyFeature1Desc: Official OSSD credits recognized by Canadian and international universities
officialOntario.keyFeature2Desc: Learn from Ontario Certified Teachers with years of teaching experience
officialOntario.keyFeature3Desc: Guided curriculum with assignments, tests, and formal evaluations
officialOntario.keyFeature4Desc: OSSD diploma accepted by top universities around the world
officialOntario.ctaDesc: Choose a grade level above to explore courses and start your journey
officialOntario.ministryDesc: Authorized to grant credits toward the Ontario Secondary School Diploma (OSSD)

hero.title: Ontario Middle School Foundation Program
hero.tagline: Build the bridge from elementary to high school success

overview.title: Program Overview
overview.body: The Middle School Foundation Program supports Grades 6 to 8 students in consolidating the academic foundations required for a smooth entry into Ontario high school pathways. Delivered on the Academic Preparation platform with an optional upgrade to an Ontario student record, the program emphasizes Academic English, mathematical reasoning, scientific inquiry, and social studies literacy.

grade6.title: Grade 6
grade6.body: Transition into middle school with stronger reading comprehension, introductory algebraic thinking, scientific investigation, and geography and history units aligned with the Ontario Curriculum.

grade7.title: Grade 7
grade7.body: Deepen academic writing, introduce integers and equations, explore ecosystems and structural science, and study Canada's historical and geographical foundations.

grade8.title: Grade 8
grade8.body: Prepare for the Grade 9 academic bridge with pre-algebra, analytical writing, optics, water systems, and Canada's history through the 20th century.

french.title: French as a Second Language
french.body: French is introduced as a Second Language starting in Grade 6. Students migrating from an international curriculum may request an assessment for placement.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Path
upgrade.body: Students may begin the year in the Academic Preparation Program and upgrade to the Official Ontario Program (Ontario student record) at any time. The $250 per course upgrade fee applies only to the subjects upgraded; full-year upgrade pricing is $600 for all 6 subjects.

cta.title: Ready to Build Your Middle School Foundation?
cta.subtitle: Choose a pathway and start your Ontario-aligned learning journey today.

hero.title: ONTARIO CURRICULUM PROGRAMS (Grades 9 to 12)
hero.subtitle: Flexible Learning Pathways
hero.tagline: Learn First. Decide Later. Succeed Safely.

highSchool.lede: We offer programs based on the Ontario Curriculum for students in Grades 9 to 12, with two flexible learning pathways.

pathwayA.title: Academic Preparation Program (Non-Credit)
pathwayA.description: The Academic Preparation Program (Non-Credit) is designed for students who aspire to pursue a rigorous academic pathway within an international educational framework. The program focuses on establishing strong academic foundations while cultivating analytical thinking, disciplined study habits, and the confidence required to succeed within the Canadian education system. Developed in alignment with Ontario curriculum standards, the program strengthens students' competencies in Academic English, mathematical reasoning, and advanced learning skills. Through personalized learning pathways and close academic mentorship, the program provides a solid transition into OSSD credit courses or EAP programs, preparing students to excel in high-quality, globally oriented academic environments.
outcomes: Transition readiness to OSSD credit courses
Transition readiness to English for Academic Purposes (EAP) programs

pathwayB.title: Ontario Secondary School Diploma (OSSD) Program
pathwayB.description: The Ontario Secondary School Diploma (OSSD) Program is a prestigious, fully accredited Canadian secondary education pathway designed for students pursuing academic excellence and global university opportunities. Delivered in alignment with Ontario curriculum standards, the program emphasizes strong academic foundations, critical thinking, and disciplined learning. Through personalized academic guidance and a structured learning environment, students are fully prepared for graduation, university admission in Canada and internationally, and long-term success within a globally recognized education system.
outcomes: Canadian and international university admission readiness

highSchool.grade9Note: The discounted tuition fee is applicable only to the one-year (8-credit) program. For single-course pricing, please view the individual courses. Students who have completed Grade 8 in their home country are not required to take French as a Second Language. An equivalent credit will be granted.
highSchool.grade10Note: The discounted tuition fee is applicable only to the one-year (8-credit) program. For single-course pricing, please view the individual courses. Students who have completed Grade 9 in their home country are not required to take French as a Second Language (Non-Credit pathway). An equivalent credit will be granted.
highSchool.grade11Note: The discounted tuition fee is applicable only to the one-year (7-credit) program. For single-course pricing, please view the individual courses. Students who have completed Grade 10 in their home country are not required to take French as a Second Language (Non-Credit pathway). An equivalent credit will be granted.
highSchool.grade12Note: MCV4U will be taken after MHF4U is completed. The discounted tuition fee is applicable only to the one-year (7-credit) program. For single-course pricing, please view the individual courses. Students who have completed Grade 10 in their home country are not required to take French as a Second Language (Non-Credit pathway). An equivalent credit will be granted.

learnFirst.title: Learn First
learnFirst.body: Start in the Academic Preparation Program to validate readiness, build confidence, and pilot the Ontario Curriculum without the pressure of a permanent academic record.

decideLater.title: Decide Later
decideLater.body: Upgrade to the Official Ontario Program at any point once the student is ready to earn credit and establish an Ontario student record.

succeedSafely.title: Succeed Safely
succeedSafely.body: Reduce the risk of a low OSSD transcript grade by demonstrating mastery in the Academic Preparation Program before formalizing a credit.

hero.title: Tuition and Fees
hero.subtitle: Transparent pricing with no hidden costs

elementary.heading: Elementary Pricing (Grades 1 to 5)
elementary.subtitle: Two pathways: Academic Preparation (self-learning) and an optional Upgrade to the Official Ontario Program (Ontario student record).
elementary.academicPrep: Academic Preparation (Non-Credit) — $75 per course (50% off $150), $325 CAD for the full-year 6-subject bundle.
elementary.upgrade: Upgrade to Ontario Official Program — $250 per course, $600 CAD per year (all 6 subjects).
elementary.legacy: Live Teacher-Led Primary Foundation — $3,500 CAD per year (premium pathway with Ontario report card).
elementary.fees: Registration Fee: $50 CAD. Entrance Test Fee: waived for Grades 1 to 5.

middle.heading: Middle School Pricing (Grades 6 to 8)
middle.subtitle: Same structure as Elementary, with a $50 CAD entrance test fee.
middle.academicPrep: Academic Preparation (Non-Credit) — $75 per course (50% off $150), $325 CAD for the full-year 6-subject bundle.
middle.upgrade: Upgrade to Ontario Official Program — $250 per course, $600 CAD per year (all 6 subjects).
middle.fees: Registration Fee: $50 CAD. Entrance Test Fee: $50 CAD (non-refundable).

high.heading: High School Pricing (Grades 9 to 12)
high.subtitle: Per-course and full-year program pricing for the 8-credit (Grades 9 and 10) and 7-credit (Grades 11 and 12) one-year programs.
high.academicPrep: Academic Preparation (Non-Credit) — From $75 per course (50% off $150).
high.official: Official Ontario Program — Per-course from $700 CAD. Full-year 8-credit program from $3,800 CAD (Grades 9 and 10). Full-year 7-credit program from $3,800 CAD (Grades 11 and 12). Contact admissions for domestic, visa, and international tier quotes.
high.fees: Registration Fee: $100 CAD. Entrance Test Fee: $50 CAD (non-refundable).

tuitionPage.feeScheduleNote: All fees are non-refundable. Refund eligibility on tuition depends on course progress — contact admissions for details.
paymentMethods: Flywire (for international payments, supports Alipay and WeChat Pay)
tuitionPage.schedule: Standard academic year: September 5, 2026 to May 30, 2027 (39 instructional weeks). Continuous enrollment is also available — you may start any day of the year.
tuitionPage.ctaSubtitle: Start your Canadian education journey today.

overview.heading: Calendar Overview
overview.winter: Winter Break: mid-December 2026 to early January 2027

schedulePage.continuousBanner: Continuous enrollment is still available. You may start any day of the year and complete your course within 12 months.
schedulePage.assessmentBody: Official Ontario students complete proctored final examinations (30% of the final grade) during scheduled assessment windows. OSSLT dates are published by the Education Quality and Accountability Office (EQAO) and communicated by EMCS guidance counselors. Midterm mark reports are issued in early February 2027.
schedulePage.transcriptBody: OUAC and OCAS applications receive automatic midterm (February 2027) and final (June 2027) mark uploads. International transcripts are released within 10 business days of final grade posting.

programCards.academicPrep: Academic Preparation Program — Self-paced, 12-month access from enrollment. May begin any day of the year.
programCards.officialHs910: Official Ontario Program (Grades 9 and 10) — Live 8-credit one-year program, September 5, 2026 to May 30, 2027.
programCards.officialHs1112: Official Ontario Program (Grades 11 and 12) — Live 7-credit one-year program, September 5, 2026 to May 30, 2027.
programCards.primaryFoundation: Primary Foundation Teacher-Led — Live 6-hour/week program, September 5, 2026 to May 30, 2027.

hero.title: Academic Preparation Program vs. Official Ontario Program
hero.subtitle: Choose the pathway that fits your goals.

assessment.official: Full assessment with proctored finals, 70% coursework + 30% final examination

focus.label: Academic Focus
focus.academic: Academic English, mathematical reasoning, scientific literacy, social studies, essential learning skills
focus.official: Full Ontario Curriculum across all eight subject groupings

progression.label: Progression
progression.academic: Upgrade path into the Official Ontario Program at any time
progression.official: Direct OSSD completion and OUAC or OCAS application

outcome.label: Outcome
outcome.official: OSSD graduation, Canadian and international university admission readiness

schedule.label: Schedule
schedule.academic: September 5, 2026 to May 30, 2027 with continuous enrollment

quiz.heading: Not sure? Take our 60-second program quiz
quiz.subtitle: Answer 4 quick questions and we will recommend the best pathway for your student.

questions.q: What grade is the student entering?
Is an Ontario student record required for school transfer or university admission?
Has the student already mastered equivalent curriculum in their home country?

options.label: Yes, they are ready for credit-bearing work

academic.title: Start with the Academic Preparation Program
academic.body: Based on your answers, we recommend beginning with the Academic Preparation Program. You can upgrade to the Official Ontario Program at any time once the student is ready to earn credit.

official.title: Enroll in the Official Ontario Program
official.body: Based on your answers, we recommend the Official Ontario Program so the student earns credit toward the OSSD from day one.

highSchool.title: Start with the High School Pathways hub
highSchool.body: Based on your answers, the student should explore both flexible High School pathways side by side. Visit the High School Pathways page to review the Grades 9 to 12 timetables.
